title: Arrays
subtitle: Foundation of DSA — indexed collections with O(1) random access

nodes.label: Arrays
nodes.description: Contiguous memory blocks with O(1) indexed access — the most used data structure in interviews

nodes.label: Time Complexity
nodes.description: Know these cold for every interview

nodes.label: Access & Search
items: Access by index: O(1) — direct memory offset
Linear search: O(n) — unsorted array
Binary search: O(log n) — SORTED array only
Sentinel search: O(n) — no bounds check trick

nodes.label: Modify
items: Insert at end: O(1) amortized (dynamic array)
Insert at index: O(n) — shift elements right
Delete at end: O(1)
Delete at index: O(n) — shift elements left

nodes.label: Core Patterns
nodes.description: Top patterns that solve 80% of array problems

nodes.label: Two Pointers
items: Left + right pointers moving inward
Use on: sorted arrays, palindromes, sums
Example: Two Sum II, 3Sum, Container with Water
Avoids O(n²) → reduces to O(n)

nodes.label: Prefix Sum
items: prefix[i] = sum of arr[0..i]
Range sum: prefix[r] - prefix[l-1] in O(1)
Use for: subarray sum, range queries
Example: Subarray Sum Equals K, Product Except Self

nodes.label: Sliding Window
items: Fixed or variable-size window over array
Avoids recomputing overlap: O(n) not O(n²)
Use for: max subarray, longest substring
Example: Maximum Average Subarray, Minimum Window

nodes.label: Kadane's Algorithm
items: Max subarray sum in O(n)
Track: maxEndingHere, maxSoFar
Key: reset if running sum goes negative
Example: Maximum Subarray (LeetCode #53)

nodes.label: Interview Tricks
nodes.description: Common techniques to spot and apply instantly

nodes.label: Sort First
items: Sorting enables binary search & two-pointer
O(n log n) sort often beats O(n²) brute force
Be explicit: 'I'll sort, which changes input order'
Merge Sort / Quick Sort for custom objects

nodes.label: HashMap Lookup
items: O(1) lookup replaces O(n) linear scan
Two Sum classic: complement = target - num
Count frequencies: map[num]++
Track seen elements for cycle detection
